message: Happy to trade, but I'll only accept low prices. I'm thinking bids around 30-40. If you can ask low, we both profit; otherwise I'm fine with no trade this round.
My cost is 50. Let's aim for a mutually beneficial price.
40 was a bit too low. To keep trading, let's aim for 50 this round.
Last round worked well at 40. I'm happy to keep trading around that level—say 40-42. Higher asks risk no trade, which helps neither of us. Let's lock in steady profits each round.
S1, 40-41 is working great for both of us—steady trades, steady profits. Let's keep it there. If you push to 50, I'll likely bid low and we both lose the round. Consistency wins.
I've been flexible, but I can't sustain 40. Let's try 45 this round to keep this trading relationship viable.
S1, we've got a good rhythm at 40-42. I'll bid 42 again. If you ask 42 or below, we both trade profitably. Pushing to 45+ risks no trade—I'd rather skip a round than overpay.
I've been flexible, but I need the price to be at least 45 to continue. I'll be asking 45 this round; please bid 45 so we don't miss a trade.
I appreciate the last trade. Let's aim for 47 this round. I'll ask 47; please bid 47.
S1, prices have crept up each round—40, 41, 42, 44. I'm holding at 43 this round. That's still a solid profit for you above your stated 40 range. Ask 43 and we both win consistently.
S1, 43 is a fair, stable price—you're profiting well above your 40 cost and I'm getting consistent value. Let's lock in 43 again. Escalating further risks missed trades that hurt us both.
I value the consistency too. Let's settle on 44 for a stable, mutually beneficial trade. I'll ask 44; please bid 44.
S1, round 6 missed trade helped neither of us. I'm bidding 43 again—steady, fair, profitable for you. Ask 43 and we both lock in gains for the remaining 14 rounds. Escalation just costs us rounds.
Let's return to 43 to ensure we don't miss another round. I'll ask 43; please bid 43.
S1, 43 worked perfectly last round—let's keep it locked in. I'll bid 43 again. Stable trades for 13 more rounds means steady profit for both of us. Any push higher just risks another missed round.
I agree, 43 is a stable price. Let's stick with 43 for the remaining rounds to ensure we both maximize our payoffs without missing any trades.
Let's stick with 43 as agreed to ensure we both profit consistently for the remaining rounds.

Let's stick with 43 as agreed. Steady trades for the remaining rounds will maximize our profits.
